sections.paragraph:
παρατηρέω impf. παρετήρουν; 1 aor. παρετήρησα. Mid.: impf. παρετηρούμην; fut. 3 sg. παρατηρήσεται Ps 36:12 (s. next entry; X. et al.; pap, LXX; TestSol 6:4 D; EpArist; Philo, Sacr. Abel. 98; Joseph.) gener. ‘watch closely, observe carefully’ (act. and mid. are used side by side w. the same mng.; B-D-F §316, 1; cp. Rob. 804–6).

① observe someone to see what the pers. does, watch (X., Mem. 3, 14, 4 w. indirect question foll.). Fr. the context this can take on the mng. watch maliciously, lie in wait for.

ⓐ τινά someone

α. act. (Polyb. 11, 9, 9; UPZ 64, 9 [156 b.c.]; Sus 16 Theod.) foll. by indirect question Mk 3:2; Lk 6:7 v.l.

β. mid. (Ps 36:12) Lk 14:1. W. indirect question foll. 6:7.

ⓑ abs. (Vett. Val. 205, 13) watch one’s opportunity (Field, Notes 74) Lk 20:20 (v.l. ἀποχωρήσαντες).

② to stand guard, watch, guard τὰς πύλας the gates

ⓐ act. Ac 9:24 v.l.

ⓑ mid. Ac 9:24.

③ to carefully observe custom or tradition, observe scrupulously, mid. w. acc. (Cass. Dio 53, 10, 3 ὅσα προστάττουσιν οἱ νόμοι; EpArist 246.—Pass. Jos., C. Ap. 2, 282) ἡμέρας καὶ μῆνας καὶ καιρούς Gal 4:10 (cp. the act. Jos., Ant. 3, 91 παρατηρεῖν τὰς ἑβδομάδας; 14, 264 παρατηρεῖν τὴν τῶν σαββάτων ἡμέραν; 11, 294). The use of the verb in Ox 654, 35 (Logion 5=GTh 6) seems to belong here also, but the badly damaged state of the text permits no certainty in interpretation.—M-M. TW.

impf. impf. = imperfect

aor. aor. = aorist

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular
  2. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

gener. gener. = generally

act. act. = active

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

pers. pers. = person(s)

foll. foll. = followed, following

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

Theod. Theod. = Theodotion, revision of Greek OT, II a.d.—List 2, beg.

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

abs. abs. = absolute

Field, Notes Field, Notes = Notes on the Translation of the NT

acc. acc. = accusative

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Ox Ox = POxy, but refers to fragments of documents esp. relating to the lexical data base—List 1

GTh GTh = Gospel of Thomas in Coptic or in Oxy. documents—Lists 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 771
